ksa_text: Knowledge of data protection law in detail, including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, and sector-specific privacy regulations, with focus on legal bases for processing, data subject rights, cross-border transfer mechanisms, compliance procedures, and enforcement precedent.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Security & Platform Strategy is a primary or secondary domain for the Director of Data Governance. This knowledge KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (director).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Security & Platform Strategy), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.493). External reviewers may question whether this specific knowledge KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Director of Data Governance versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NIST CSF, CMU SEI enterprise security frameworks, and CDAIO job market data consistently include security and platform strategy oversight for senior data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Security & Platform Strategy appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Director of Data Governance.

decision_rationale: Security & Platform Strategy knowledge KSA is within scope for the Director of Data Governance at the director tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
